LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION REGARDING DELTA BAY WATERPROOFING DUTY TO DEFEND IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT THE TRIAL JUDGE SHOULD RULE ON THE DUTY TO DEFEND ISSUE. DENYING THE MOTION WILL AVOID THE PRACTICAL DIFFICULTIES OF SORTING OUT MULTIPLE, AND POTENTIALLY CONFLICTING, DUTIES TO ASSUME THE ACTIVE DEFENSE OF LITIGATION IN PROGRESS. SEE CRAWFORD V. WEATHER SHIELD MFG. INC. (2008) 44 CAL.4TH 541, 565, FN.12. PLAINTIFF IS NOT PREJUDICED BECAUSE IT ADMITS THAT IT IS CURRENTLY RECEIVING A DEFENSE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE FRAMCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S DUTY TO DEFEND IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT THE TRIAL JUDGE SHOULD RULE ON THE DUTY TO DEFEND ISSUE. DENYING THE MOTION WILL AVOID THE PRACTICAL DIFFICULTIES OF SORTING OUT MULTIPLE, AND POTENTIALLY CONFLICTING, DUTIES TO ASSUME THE ACTIVE DEFENSE OF LITIGATION IN PROGRESS. SEE CRAWFORD V. WEATHER SHIELD MFG. INC. (2008) 44 CAL.4TH 541, 565, FN.12. PLAINTIFF IS NOT PREJUDICED BECAUSE IT ADMITS THAT IT IS CURRENTLY RECEIVING A DEFENSE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, IS GRANTED AND ITS REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2500 IS ALSO GRANTED. INTERVENOR ST. PAUL FIRE & MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY'S ARGUMENT THAT ITS E-SERVICE OR SERVICE BY MAIL OF ITS COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION ON COUNSEL FOR ONEBEACON SUFFICED AS SUFFICIENT SERVICE ON ALL HEATING IS FRIVOLOUS AND WARRANTS IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS PER CCP 128.5. ONEBEACON AND ALL HEATING ARE TWO SEPARATE PARTIES. SERVICE ON ONE IS NOT SERVICE ON THE OTHER. BECAUSE ALL HEATING HAS NEVER APPEARED IN THE CASE, ENTRY OF ITS DEFAULT COULD ONLY OCCUR IF ALL HEATING WAS SERVED IN A MANNER PERMITTED FOR SERVING A SUMMONS AND THE TIME TO RESPOND ELAPSED WITHOUT A PROPER RESPONSE. BECAUSE THAT DID NOT OCCUR, THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST ALL HEATING MUST BE SET ASIDE. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention On Behalf Of Defendant And Cross-Defendant All Heating Sheet Metal, Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Angela Zanin-Wog, of The Aguilera Law Group, Tel. 213.929.1330, for Intervenor St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Company;
Mark S. Dove, of Morrow & White, Tel. 714.979.7999, for First Specialty Insurance Corporation.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling in its entirety. INTERVENOR FIRST SPECIALITY INSURANCE COMPANY Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention On Behalf Of Defendant And Cross-Defendant All Heating Sheet Metal, Inc. is granted. First Specialty has shown that it is entitled to intervene to seek to set aside the default entered against All Heating & Sheet Metal, Inc. and to protect its own interests as an insurer of All Heating. All of the arguments asserted by Intervenor St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Company in opposition to this motion lack merit. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Angela Zanin-Wog, of The Aguilera Law Group, Tel. 213.929.1330, for Intervenor St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Company;
Mark S. Dove, of Morrow & White, Tel. 714.979.7999, for First Specialty Insurance Corporation.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court modifies its tentative ruling as to the request for sanctions only. INTERVENOR FIRST SPECIALITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is granted and its request for monetary sanctions in the amount of $2500 is also granted. Intervenor St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Company's argument that its e-service or service by mail of its complaint in intervention on counsel for OneBeacon sufficed as sufficient service on All Heating is frivolous and warrants imposition of sanctions per CCP 128.5. OneBeacon and All Heating are two separate parties. Service on one is not service on the other. Because All Heating has never appeared in the case, entry of its default could only occur if All Heating was served in a manner permitted for serving a summons and the time to respond elapsed without a proper response. Because that did not occur, the default entered against All Heating must be set aside. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Clayton Cook, Esq., 916.929.1481, for North Bay Drywall & Plastering, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT FRAMCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Defendant Framco fails to maintain its summary adjudication burden. Church Street Housing Associates never agreed to waive that statutory statute of limitations as to Framco. There is no authority holding that a subcontractor can bind an owner by entering into an agreement with the general contractor. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Complaint Of Church Street Housing Associates
Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Gunn, Esq., 415.693.2070, for Cahill Contractors, Inc. and Church Street Housing Association.
Edward Morrison, Jr., Esq., 213.356.5500, Val Meneses, Esq., 619.233.8591, and Carlos Cabral, Esq., 805.648.5111, for Framco Construction Company.
Julyn Park, Esq., 408.416.2929, for Milgard Manufacturing, Inc.
Angela Zanin-Wog, Esq., 714.384.6600, for St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Company.
Bryan Bjorge, Esq., 916.851.3792, for A&B Painting, Esq.,
Clayton Cook, Esq., 916.929.1481, for North Bay Drywall & Plastering, Inc.
Jason Murphy, Esq., 619.338.0300, for Magalit & Petrucione Construction.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT FRAMCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Complaint Of Church Street Housing Associates is denied. Defendant Framco fails to maintain its summary adjudication burden. The court sustains Church Street Housing Association's objection to the Morrison declaration. Mr. Morrison does not properly authenticate the cited exhibits. In addition, there exists a triable issue of material fact when Church Street housing Association discovered or reasonably should have discovered the defects in this case. See Haddix Dec. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Adrianne Duncan, Esq,. 925.734.0990, for Plaintiff Cahill Contractors, Inc.
Val Meneses, Esq., 619.233.8591, for Defendant Framco Construction Company.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is granted. Cross-complainant Cahill's cash settlement amount of $500,000 is within the Tech-Bilt v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, "reasonable range" or "ballpark" standard for good faith. Non-settling defendants may receive benefit from the offset of Cahill's settlement contribution, despite its assignment of indemnification rights to plaintiff. Order signed in open court. Off Calendar Jan-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHAD TAPP, ESQ., 415.227.2343, FOR NORTH BAY DRYWALL & PLASTERING.
MARK DOVE, ESQ., 714.979.7999, FOR A&B PAINTING, INC.
EDWARD MORRISON JR, ESQ., 213.356.5500, AND JOHN MILLAR, ESQ., 619.233.8591, FOR FRAMCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY.
ROXANNE IRANI, ESQ., 714.426.4600 X7743, FOR PENNSYLVANIA GENERAL INSURANCE CO.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Magalit & Petrucione Construction'S Duty To Defend is denied without prejudice. The Court determines that the trial judge should rule on the duty to defend issue. Denying the motion will avoid the practical difficulties of sorting out multiple, and potentially conflicting, duties to assume the active defense of litigation in progress. See Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, fn.12. Plaintiff is not prejudiced because it admits that it is currently receiving a defense. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
